cards.front: What does FAAFO stand for — the five dimensions along which vibe coding creates value?
cards.back: Fast, Ambitious, Autonomous, Fun, and Optionality.

cards.front: Who coined the term “vibe coding,” and when?
cards.back: Andrej Karpathy, in a viral tweet in February 2025: “fully give in to the vibes, embrace exponentials, and forget that the code even exists.”

cards.front: Why is there no “B” (for better) in FAAFO?
cards.back: Vibe coding does not automatically make your code better — that remains the developer's responsibility.

cards.front: In the Adidas 700-developer case study, what productivity gains did the second AI pilot deliver?
cards.back: 70% of developers saw 20–30% productivity gains, and engineers reported about 50% more “Happy Time” (time spent on work they actually want to do).

cards.front: What is Brooks's Law, and why does it happen?
cards.back: Adding manpower to a late software project makes it later (Dr. Fred Brooks, The Mythical Man-Month) — communication lines grow exponentially with team size: 3 people have 3 lines, 10 people have 45.
